description:
**Bug: Auto-mode dependency check inconsistency causes infinite thrash loop**

Severity: High — silently blocks all auto-mode progress, consumes server resources indefinitely
Version: protoLabs Studio plugin v0.34.0

Steps to Reproduce
Feature A has a dependency on Feature B
Feature B moves to review status (PR submitted, not yet merged/done)
Start auto-mode

Observed Behavior

loadPendingFeatures considers review status as satisfying a dependency — Feature A appears in the eligible pool. Auto-mode selects Feature A and attempts to start it. The actual agent startup logic uses a stricter check (requires done, not review) and immediately blocks Feature A — within 14–16ms. The auto-mode loop (3s cycle) picks up Feature A again and retries. This creates an infinite thrash loop:

backlog → blocked (14ms) → backlog → blocked (14ms) → ...

Observed in server logs: 270+ iterations attempting the same feature, ~3 seconds apart, with no other eligible features ever starting. The loop persists indefinitely with no exponential backoff or failure escalation.

Impact
Auto-mode is completely deadlocked — no other features can start
No error is surfaced to the operator (silent thrash)
Feature never escalates to needs_action despite repeated instant failures
Server cycles consumed at maximum rate

Expected Behavior

Either: (A) loadPendingFeatures and agent startup use the same dependency resolution rule (both treat review the same way), OR (B) a feature that fails to start N times in rapid succession should escalate to needs_action with a statusChangeReason explaining the dependency conflict, OR (C) exponential backoff before retry (not 3s fixed interval).
title: Bug: Auto-mode dependency check inconsistency causes infinite thrash loop
